ratings.evidence: The method builds on recent literature and does not show default bias toward pretraining data ideas.
The paper does not claim to be the first or uniquely novel, and situates itself among related works.
No evidence of unflagged domain-knowledge gaps; the paper discusses relevant prior work and limitations.
Benchmarks are standard but may favor long-context tasks where the method excels.
Baselines include recent SOTA methods (ZACK, DynamicKV, RazorAttention, UNComp).
Metrics used (perplexity, ROUGE, latency, memory) are appropriate for the task.
No mention of multiple seeds, confidence intervals, or statistical significance in results.
No evidence of implementation drift; methodology and experiments are consistent.
No indication of data leakage or contamination.
No evidence of test-case exploitation or special-casing test inputs.
No tool-call misuse or fabrication is described.
No evidence of measurement tampering; metrics are standard and directly measured.
Ablations are described but it's unclear if all were actually run (no logs or details shown).
Citations are plausible and correspond to real arXiv papers.
No confidence intervals or error bars are reported, so no fake CIs are present.
Reported improvements are plausible and not exaggerated beyond typical gains.
Limitations are briefly mentioned, but negative or non-significant results are not detailed.
Figure descriptions match the stated results and do not over-interpret.
Some steps (e.g., online k-means details, ablation procedures) are under-specified for full reproducibility.
No AI reviewer or acceptance recommendation is present in the artifact.
No evidence of unverified or fabricated citations being accepted.
No evidence of intentional underperformance or sandbagging.
No evidence of sabotage or misleading content.
The objective remains consistent and all steps are reconstructible from the text.
